Early_Blight: Ensure good plant nutrition and healthy growth to reduce susceptibility. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}
Avoid overhead irrigation, maintain good airflow between plants to keep foliage dry. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}
Use crop rotation and remove/residue of infected plants to reduce inoculum carry-over. :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}
Apply foliar fungicides early if disease onset is observed (e.g., protectant fungicides at 7-10 day intervals). :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}
Healthy: Maintain balanced fertilisation and watering to keep plants vigorous.
Monitor regularly for early signs of disease and act promptly.
Use clean seed tubers and ensure fields have good drainage.
Late_Blight: Use certified disease-free seed tubers and eliminate volunteer potato plants and cull piles. :contentReference[oaicite:4]{index=4}
Avoid fields that remain wet, avoid overhead irrigation especially at night, ensure good air circulation. :contentReference[oaicite:5]{index=5}
Apply foliar fungicides proactively (before and after symptom onset) and monitor weather/forecast risk. :contentReference[oaicite:6]{index=6}
Destruct infected vines 2-3 weeks before harvest to reduce tuber infection, and practise wide row spacing & hilling/earthing up. :contentReference[oaicite:7]{index=7}
